title: PutObject, Metadata, Checksums, and Encryption Defaults · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs putobject, metadata, checksums, and encryption defaults from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for single-object upload and integrity evidence record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Own and close input bodies, preserve cancellation, bound bytes, and distinguish request completion from later business publication.
Select supported checksum algorithms, send full-object evidence, validate service response, and retain an application integrity record.
Set and preserve Content-Type, Content-Disposition, Cache-Control, encoding, language, custom metadata, and tags during replacements and copies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Write Result Evidence
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 1231 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove accepted bytes, checksum, metadata, and encryption without using ETag as a universal digest; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record bucket, key, version, checksum, size, request ID, encryption, policy decision, and changed-read verification without logging secrets.

Inspect the Write Result Evidence trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Write Result Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for record bucket, key, version, checksum, size, request id, encryption, policy decision, and changed-read verification without logging secrets. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Write Result Evidence, keep Write Result Evidence connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record bucket, key, version, checksum, size, request ID, encryption, policy decision, and changed-read verification without logging secrets.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Write Result Evidence, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Write Result Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Write Result Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, custody, integrity, range, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 3564 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove accepted bytes, checksum, metadata, and encryption without using ETag as a universal digest; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record bucket, key, version, checksum, size, request ID, encryption, policy decision, and changed-read verification without logging secrets.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Write Result Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: The object key is enough evidence to audit a write.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using record and bucket plus specific case evidence.
